name: Fort Kochi
description: Colonial-era waterfront quarter with Chinese fishing nets, Dutch/Portuguese architecture, and St. Francis Church.
keywords: fort kochi;chinese fishing nets;st francis church

name: Cherai Beach
description: Golden-sand beach on Vypeen Island with 15km coastline, coconut groves, paddy fields; occasional dolphin sightings.

name: Mattancherry Palace (Dutch Palace)
description: Portuguese-built palace, later renovated by the Dutch, with murals depicting the Ramayana and Mahabharata.

name: Jew Town & Paradesi Synagogue
description: Antique-shop-lined street in Mattancherry, home to the Paradesi Synagogue (built 1567), among the oldest in the Commonwealth.

name: Marine Drive
description: Waterfront promenade along the backwaters in the heart of Kochi city, a popular evening hangout.

name: Bolgatty Palace
description: Dutch-built palace (1744) on Bolgatty Island, one of the oldest Dutch palaces outside the Netherlands, now a heritage hotel.

name: Chottanikkara Bhagavathy Temple
description: Revered Devi temple where the goddess is worshipped as Saraswati (morning), Lakshmi (noon), Durga (evening); known for healing traditions.

name: Hill Palace Museum
description: Kerala's largest archaeological museum, former residence of the Kochi royal family, on sprawling palace grounds in Tripunithura.

name: Wonderla Kochi
description: Large amusement and water park with thrill rides, ISO 14001 certified, one of the biggest entertainment parks in South India.

name: Thattekkad Bird Sanctuary
description: First bird sanctuary in Kerala, on the banks of the Periyar River; renowned birding site with 250+ recorded species.
google_maps_name: Salim Ali Bird Sanctuary, Thattekkad, Kerala

name: Bhoothathankettu Dam
description: Dam site with boating facilities in dense forest near Kothamangalam, popular picnic spot near Thattekkad sanctuary.

name: Kalady
description: Birthplace of philosopher Adi Shankaracharya (8th century), with shrines on the Periyar riverbank.

name: Munambam Beach
description: Quieter fishing-harbour beach ideal for kite flying and sunset views, less crowded than Cherai.

name: Paniyeli Poru
description: Rocky riverside picnic spot on the Periyar where a natural bottleneck creates rapids, on the edge of Thattekkad forest.

name: Mangalavanam Bird Sanctuary
description: Rare mangrove ecosystem and bird sanctuary in the heart of Kochi city, near Ernakulam Junction.

name: Kumbalangi Village
description: India's first model fishing/tourism village — Chinese-net fishing demos, clam collection, coir-making, backwater canoe rides.

name: Pallipuram Fort
description: Oldest surviving European fort in India, built by the Portuguese in 1503 on Vypeen Island; hexagonal laterite structure.

name: St. George Orthodox Church, Kadamattom
description: Ancient Indian Orthodox church near Kolenchery, linked to Bishop Mar Sabor and Saint Thomas Christian heritage.
keywords: kadamattom church;st george orthodox church kerala

name: Kumarakom
description: Cluster of islands on Vembanad Lake with houseboats, paddy fields, and backwater cruising — Kottayam's flagship destination.

name: Kumarakom Bird Sanctuary
description: 14-hectare sanctuary on Vembanad Lake, a major stop for migratory birds from the Himalayas to Siberia.

name: Ilaveezhapoonchira
description: Hill station near Melukavu, ringed by three hillocks, known for sunrise/sunset views and a monsoon-formed seasonal lake.

name: Vagamon
description: Hill station at 1100m with grass-covered hills, tea plantations, meadows, and Thangal/Murugan/Kurisumala hills.

name: Poonjar Palace
description: Palace on the Pala-Erattupetta route reflecting the regal architecture of the erstwhile Poonjar principality.

name: Thazhathangadi Juma Masjid
description: One of the oldest mosques in India (~1,000 years old), an important heritage landmark in Kottayam town.

name: Vennimala
description: Hill in Puthuppally village linked to Ramayana legend, formerly dense forest, now a serene picnic and viewpoint spot.

name: Erumeli
description: Gateway town to Sabarimala; known for the Vavar Mosque and its unique Hindu-Muslim shared pilgrimage tradition.

name: Kurisumala
description: Renowned Christian pilgrim centre 3km from Vagamon, set on a scenic hillock with a monastery.

name: Aruvikuzhi Waterfalls
description: Five-step waterfall near Erattupetta, a popular picnic spot and film shooting location.

name: Bay Island Driftwood Museum
description: Museum in Kumarakom displaying sculptures crafted from driftwood collected along the backwaters.

name: Vaikom Mahadeva Temple
description: Ancient Shiva temple, one of Kerala's most important; site of the historic 1924-25 Vaikom Satyagraha.

name: Pathiramanal Island
description: Small island in the middle of Vembanad Lake, accessible only by boat, home to rare migratory birds.

name: Illikkal Kallu
description: Monolith rock formation on Illikkal Mala reached via a scenic hairpin drive and hike; misty viewpoint.

name: Munnar
description: Iconic hill station at 1600m at the confluence of three mountain streams, famed for tea plantations and colonial-era charm.

name: Eravikulam National Park
description: Home to the endangered Nilgiri Tahr and, once every 12 years, the blue Neelakurinji bloom; rolling shola grasslands.

name: Mattupetty Dam
description: Scenic dam near Munnar with boating on the reservoir and a nearby Indo-Swiss dairy/livestock farm.

name: Top Station
description: Highest point on the Munnar-Kodaikanal road (1700m) with panoramic Western Ghats and Tamil Nadu valley views.

name: Periyar Wildlife Sanctuary (Thekkady)
description: 925 sq km tiger reserve around Periyar Lake — elephants, boat safaris, and forest treks; one of Kerala's premier wildlife destinations.

name: Idukki Arch Dam
description: 554-ft arch dam across the Kuravan and Kurathi hills on the Periyar River — Asia's first, world's second of its kind.

name: Devikulam
description: Hill town near Munnar with the Sita Devi Lake and cool climate, part of the old British-era tea belt.

name: Marayoor
description: Sandalwood forests and rare Neolithic dolmens (muniyaras) in Kerala's only naturally growing sandalwood belt; also known for jaggery.

name: Anamudi Peak
description: Highest peak in South India (2,695m), inside Eravikulam National Park; permit-based trekking.

name: Chinnar Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Rain-shadow thorny scrub forest sanctuary — distinct from Kerala's typical wet forests, home to the endangered Giant Grizzled Squirrel.

name: Ramakkalmedu
description: Windmill-topped hill station on the Kerala-Tamil Nadu border with strong year-round winds and panoramic views.

name: Vandanmedu
description: Kerala's biggest cardamom auction centre near Kumily, with plantation walks and colonial-era buildings.

name: Thommankuthu Waterfalls
description: Seven-step cascading waterfall dropping over 300ft, popular for trekking between the tiers.

name: Peermedu (Peerumedu)
description: Plantation hill town at 914m rich in coffee, tea, pepper, cardamom, and rubber, en route to Thekkady.

name: Gavi
description: Eco-tourism forest destination encircled by tea estates, promoted via Kerala Forest Development Corporation permits.

name: Athirappilly Falls
description: Kerala's largest waterfall (80ft tall, 330ft wide) on the Chalakudy River, dubbed the 'Niagara of India'.

name: Vazhachal Falls
description: Milky-textured falls on the Chalakudy River, 5km from Athirappilly, set within dense evergreen forest.

name: Vadakkunnathan Temple
description: Ancient Shiva temple on a hillock in the heart of Thrissur city, epicentre of the Thrissur Pooram festival; a UNESCO tentative site.

name: Guruvayur Sree Krishna Temple
description: One of Kerala's most important Krishna temples, drawing pilgrims nationwide; known for elephant processions and daily rituals.

name: Shakthan Thampuran Palace
description: Heritage palace-museum in Thrissur city showcasing the royal history of the Kochi kingdom's ruler Shakthan Thampuran.

name: Kerala Kalamandalam
description: Government-recognized university of performing arts on the banks of the Bharathapuzha River, home to Kathakali, Koodiyattam, Mohiniyattam.

name: Peechi Dam & Wildlife Sanctuary
description: Dam commissioned 1959 on the Manali River, focal point of the Peechi-Vazhani Wildlife Sanctuary; boating and picnicking.

name: Chavakkad Beach
description: Hidden-gem coastal town beach near Guruvayur with golden sands, popular for winter sunsets and couples.

name: Snehatheeram Beach
description: 'Beach of love' near Guruvayur, featured in the Malayalam classic film Chemmeen; family-friendly with a light park.

name: Punnathur Kotta (Elephant Sanctuary)
description: Former palace fort near Guruvayur that now houses the temple's ceremonial elephants; a favourite family stop.

name: Thrissur Zoo & State Museum
description: Combined zoo, archaeological museum, and art museum near Vadakkunnathan Temple; a family-friendly cultural stop.

name: Kole Wetlands
description: Below-sea-level wetland ecosystem supplying ~40% of Kerala's rice needs and acting as the region's natural drainage system.

name: Silver Storm Amusement Park
description: Amusement and water park at Vettilapara near Athirappilly, popular family day-trip combined with the falls.
google_maps_name: Silver Storm Amusement Park, Athirappilly, Thrissur
keywords: silver storm amusement park;athirappilly water park

name: Chimmony Wildlife Sanctuary & Dam
description: Dam and sanctuary offering trekking and boating, an ideal picnic spot away from the crowds.

name: Cheraman Juma Masjid
description: Believed to be the first mosque built in India (629 CE), in the historic port town of Kodungallur.
keywords: cheraman juma masjid;first mosque in india
